answer: Microsoft's fiscal year 2025 revenue was $281.724 billion, a 14.93% increase from 2024. This growth was driven by strong performance in cloud and AI sectors. The company's Intelligent Cloud division saw a 26% revenue increase.

results.title: Microsoft Closes Fiscal Year with $281.7B Revenue, Driven by ...
results.snippet: ## Microsoft Closes Fiscal Year with $281.7B Revenue, Driven by Cloud and AI Growth. has announced the following results for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, as compared to the corresponding period of last fiscal year:. “We closed out the fiscal year with a strong quarter, highlighted by Microsoft Cloud revenue reaching $46.7 billion, up 27% (up 25% in constant currency) year-over-year,” said Amy Hood, executive vice president and chief financial officer of Microsoft. Revenue in Productivity and Business Processes was $33.1 billion and increased 16% (up 14% in constant currency), with the following business highlights:. Revenue in Intelligent Cloud was $29.9 billion and increased 26% (up 25% in constant currency), with the following business highlights:. Revenue in More Personal Computing was $13.5 billion and increased 9%, with the following business highlights:. Microsoft (Nasdaq “MSFT”) creates platforms and tools powered by AI to deliver innovative solutions that meet the evolving needs of our customers. ### Boehringer Ingelheim Adds London AI Center to Global Computational Innovation Network.

results.title: Microsoft Corp (MSFT) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights
results.snippet: Total Revenue: $281 billion, up 15% year over year. Operating Income: Over $128 billion, up 17% year over year. Q4 Revenue: $76.4 billion, up 18

results.title: $281b From One Customer | Tomasz Tunguz
results.snippet: > “We are only at the beginning phases of AI diffusion & already Microsoft has built an AI business that is larger than some of our biggest franchises.”. CEO Satya Nadella captures Microsoft’s Q2 FY2026 earnings in a sentence. Azure grew 39%, a slight deceleration from Q1’s 40%, not because demand softened but because Microsoft ran out of capacity to sell. Microsoft Cloud revenue crossed $51b in Q2, up 26%, so AI-specific revenue remains a fraction of the total. Microsoft spent $37.5b in Q2 FY2026 on CapEx, up from $34.9b the prior quarter & $34.2b at Amazon. Microsoft’s commercial remaining performance obligation surged 110% to $625b in Q2, with an average duration of two and a half years. * Microsoft FY26 Q2 Press Release. * Microsoft Q1 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript. * Microsoft Q2 2026 Earnings - CNBC. * Microsoft’s AI Business Surges to $10 Billion - PYMNTS. * Microsoft Q1 FY'26 Cloud Supply Constraints - Directions on Microsoft.
